Mod.name: Spell Push
Mod.description:
By default, Noita swaps two spells whenever you drop one onto an occupied slot. Spell Push replaces that with insert-and-push: the spell you're dragging takes the target slot, and whatever was there slides over to make room instead of trading places with it.
Holding Left Alt while dropping a spell reverts to Noita's default drag-and-drop behaviour. This can be inverted in the mod settings menu to make default noita behaviour the norm, and activate the mod with left alt instead.
If there's an open slot to the right of where you dropped, everything between moves right by one. If there's no room to the right but there is to the left, everything moves left instead. If the wand is completely full and the spell came from somewhere else (your inventory, another wand), it falls back to a normal swap. Rearranging spells within the same wand always works, even on a full wand — the slot you dragged from becomes the opening.
Example:
A full 3-slot wand holding [A, B, C]. Drag C onto slot 1, and instead of swapping A and C, you get [C, A, B] — everything just moves over by one.
What it doesn't touch
The spell inventory keeps vanilla swap behaviour — this mod only changes how wand slots respond to drops.
The mod is mouse-only for now (no controller support). It works by adjusting existing drag-and-drop behaviour.
